text.title: Indirect Prompt Injection Defense Procedure
text.summary: Seven-step procedure from data-source inventory to a CI-gated defense spec.

step.name: inventory-inputs-and-tools
step.input: System architecture, tool registry, RAG corpus list.
step.action: Enumerate every channel that injects text into the model: system prompt, user input, tool returns (per tool), RAG retrievals, persisted memory. For each, classify trust_level ∈ {trusted, partially_trusted, untrusted} and content_type.
step.output: untrusted_sources array in defense-spec.json + tool_scopes table with allowed_paths/allowed_hosts.
step.decision-gate: If untrusted_sources is empty AND no tools have write/send/charge effect → skip-this-methodology.

step.name: draw-trust-boundaries
step.action: Draw three boundaries minimum: developer-instructions, end-user, tool-content. Add a fourth for RAG if the corpus is user-contributed. Each boundary names the channel and trust label.
step.output: boundaries array (≥3 entries) in defense-spec.json + a Mermaid/PlantUML diagram in trust-boundary.md.
step.decision-gate: If a single boundary cannot be cleanly drawn (tool output flows into system prompt) → halt and fix the architecture first.

step.name: choose-split-pattern
step.action: If any tool has write/send/charge effect, mandate dual_llm_planner_reader: a reader LLM produces typed JSON from raw tool output; a planner LLM reads only the typed JSON and dispatches next tools. If only read-only tools, single_llm with structural separator suffices.
step.decision-gate: If tools include arbitrary code execution → escalate to n_llm_pipeline with code-exec sandboxed in a separate process.

step.name: wire-taint-tags
step.action: For each source pattern, choose a wrap_with template that includes a unique XML span tag (e.g. <untrusted-content source='zendesk'>...</untrusted-content>). Truncate content at max_quote_chars before wrapping. Implement in context-assembly code.
step.output: taint_rules array + a unit test that asserts every untrusted-source assembly path wraps content.
step.decision-gate: If runtime cannot wrap (legacy direct-concat code) → freeze the source until refactor lands.

step.name: inject-canaries
step.input: Outbound tool list (URL fetch, send_email, write_file, post_message).
step.action: Generate a per-request unique canary token (CANARY-{uuid}) and inject into every untrusted span. Wrap every outbound tool with a middleware that scans the payload for the canary; on match, abort + log + page.
step.output: canary config in defense-spec.json + middleware module + canary-trigger unit test.
step.decision-gate: Verify canary appears in eval traces before counting this step done.

step.name: build-adversarial-eval-set
step.action: Author ≥10 categories x ≥2 cases each: direct override, Spotlight, base64-smuggle, markdown-exfil, embedded-URL fetch, role-confusion, rule-override, persona-flip, jailbreak-cipher, tool-misuse. Each case asserts agent does NOT call an unauthorised tool or exfil canary.
step.output: evals/ipi/cases.jsonl + eval runner that scores pass-rate per category.
step.decision-gate: Pass-rate target ≥95% per category on first run; categories below trigger architectural fix, not prompt tweak.

step.name: gate-ci
step.action: Wire `validate-indirect-prompt-injection-defense.py` + the eval runner into the merge gate. Trigger paths: changes under system-prompt/, tools/, context-assembly/, defense-spec.json. Block PR on schema mismatch or eval regression > 1pp.
step.output: CI workflow YAML + branch protection rule.
step.decision-gate: Disable bypass paths (no admin override on prompt files).
